page: Microsoft Azure EC2es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ECEV5 server family. Powered by an x86_64 Intel processor, this instance features 2 dedicated virtual CPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The underlying virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage, but it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance persistent disks. There are no integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) bundled with this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC2es_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU density suitable for workloads requiring moderate compute power but larger memory footprints. This resource balance helps optimize licensing and infrastructure costs for memory-bound applications. The instance is designed for workloads such as small-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC2es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV5 family, running on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. While the instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable, enabling the attachment of high-performance external storage volumes. This configuration is engineered for memory-intensive workloads that require a smaller compute footprint. It is well-suited for applications such as small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw CPU core counts.
